project.description:
UI Expression Intents — the ui.expression Facet's vocabulary. An Expression Intent describes something intended to be expressed in the UI, decoupled from any physical realization (colour, shape, sound). It is the portable core: application code names an intent; a Realizer (a later module) projects it to per-substrate form.
An intent is a (usage x problem-concept x value) cell, so this module depends on homing-problem-realm: a domain-bound intent references a domain node there. The first family is glance.phase-status.* — the Glance usage's phase-status family — binding to realm.plan.Status and referencing the canonical realm.plan.PhaseState. The Facet-native branch (emphasis / surface, and the presentation-role layer) is deferred until its first family is built.
